chunks.text: ORDER SHEET THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANA C. P No.S-387 of 2025 Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on office objections ‘A’. 2. For hearing of main case. 24-10-2025 Mr. Iltaf Hussain Khokhar, Advocate a/w petitioner. Mr. Aftab Nek Muhammad Bhutto, Assistant A.G a/w SIP Ali Dost on behalf of SSP, Larkana, Altaf Hussain, SHO, PS Seehar, SIP Altaf Hussain of PS Steel Town Karachi and SIP Shahzado of PS Shah Latif Town Karachi. Mr. Sardar Ali Solangi, D.P.G for the State. Respondents No.15,17 and 20 present in person. ************** Mr. Kamran Ahmed Gorar, Advocate, holds brief on behalf of Mr. Habibullah Ghouri, Advocate, and submits that the latter is out of station. He files Vakalatnama of Mr. Ghouri on behalf of respondents No.15, 17, and 20, which is taken on record. Learned Assistant Advocate General files comments/reports on behalf of official respondents No.2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 9, and 10, which are also taken on record. Copies thereof have been supplied to learned counsel for the petitioner. Learned counsel for the petitioner contends that the official respondents, at the behest of private respondents, are allegedly harassing the petitioner and her family. He, therefore, prays for protection. Learned counsel holding brief for Mr. Habibullah Ghouri submits that private respondents No.15, 17, and 20 are present in Court and they undertake not to cause any harassment to the petitioner and to act strictly in accordance with law. In their comments, the official respondents have denied the allegations of harassment and have stated that they neither have harassed nor intend to harass the petitioner. It is further submitted that there exists enmity between the parties and that FIRs have been lodged by both sides, including two FIRs registered against petitioner's son.

In their comments, the official respondents have denied the allegations of harassment and have stated that they neither have harassed nor intend to harass the petitioner. It is further submitted that there exists enmity between the parties and that FIRs have been lodged by both sides, including two FIRs registered against petitioner's son. Learned Assistant Advocate General submits that the official respondents are duty-bound to act in accordance with law and shall provide protection to the petitioner, if and when required. Learned counsel for the petitioner, after going through the comments/statements filed by the official respondents, does not press the petition on merits but reiterates his request for protection. In view of the above, this petition stands disposed of with directions to the official respondents to act strictly in accordance with law, to ensure that no harassment is caused to the petitioner, and to provide protection to her as and when required. Let a copy of this order be transmitted to the learned Additional Advocate General, Sindh, and to the official respondents concerned for information and necessary compliance. Judge Irshad Ali M/Steno
